description: A conceptual spatial diagram illustrating a multi-station Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) or ground motion monitoring network. Four tower-like ground stations, each equipped with a receiver and an associated local 3D Cartesian coordinate system (represented by blue x, y, z axes), are depicted on a flat, light blue plane. Orange dashed lines connect each of these four ground stations to a central target point. This central target is marked by a green ellipse and an independent 3D Cartesian coordinate system (represented by purple x, y, z axes). The diagram demonstrates the principle of determining the precise position and orientation of the central target point through measurements or observations from the surrounding network of reference stations. The image is labelled (c) in the bottom right corner.
